fields.definition: ABAC is a transversal, transactional information system allowing for the execution and monitoring of all budgetary and accounting operations by the Commission, an Agency or Institution.

All data by the Broadband Coverage in Europe study is available at this link and also at Eurostat, table ISOC_CBT: Broadband internet coverage by technology

Data collected by Omdia and Point Topic and verified by the national regulatory authorities (by data experts appointed by the members of the Communications Committee in every Member State).

fields.label: Pilot of new Digital Skills Indicators
fields.definition: Pilot work carried out by DG CONNECT/F4 in relation to action 62 of the Digital Agenda to propose "EU-wide indicators of digital competence". These indicators have been piloted on the 2012 microdata from the Eurostat community survey on ICT usage by Households/Individuals. It is proposed for regular implementation starting with the 2015 survey.

fields.label: European Commission services, through the Communications Committee (COCOM)
fields.definition: Methodology: The broadband take-up indicators are collected via the Communications Committee (COCOM), which consists of the relevant representatives of National Regulatory Authorities in the Member States. The data collection is carried out by the European Commission’s services, by sending a questionnaire in an excel format to the relevant representatives. The data collection takes place between October and December of the respective year that is followed by two or three validation rounds. The reference date of the collected data is the 1st of July of the respective year. The share of broadband lines in a certain speed category is computed by the European Commission’s services based on the collected number of broadband lines.

fields.label: ESSIE Survey about ICT in Education - SMART 2010/0039
fields.definition: The ESSIE Survey about ICT in Education - SMART 2010/0039 - is based on over 190,000 filled questionnaires from students, teachers and head teachers in 27 countries, collected during school-year 2011-12  (mainly between September and December 2011), with the purpose of benchmarking access, use and attitudes to IC Technologies in Europe's schools (Primary; Lower secondary; Upper secondary, both general and vocational).
fields.note: Data for 2006 comes from a previous study for the EC by Empirica, only partially comparable.

fields.label: Point Topic Study on broadband coverage

fields.label: Study on "Quality of Broadband Services in the EU” SMART 2010/0036
fields.definition: Study on "Quality of Broadband Services in the EU” (SMART 2010/0036). For the purposes of this study 9,104 households across the European Union were given a specially configured hardware device (SamKnows Whitebox), which runs a series of purpose-built tests to measure every aspect of Internet performance. The sample is designed to represent tha main fixed broadband services, used by consumers and available all around the country.

fields.label: Analysis of publications and patents of ICT research in FP7
fields.definition: Analysis conducted by DG CONNECT Unit F4, based on results of the study SMART 2011/0039 which main results are published in: Jacob, J., Sanditov, B., Smirnov, E., Wintjes, R., Surpatean, A., Notten, A., & Sasso, S. (Forthcoming). Analysis of publications and patents of ICT research in FP7. Brussels, European Commission.

Digital decade e-Health indicators reports, covering the baseline assessment on EU27 countries’ (plus Iceland and Norway) state-of-play towards delivering the Digital Decade Policy Programme 2030 e-health target of 100% EU citizens’ access to electronic health records by 2030.

Dealroom.co is a global provider of data and intelligence on startups and tech ecosystems.

Access dataset: app.dealroom.co

fields.label: Edge Observatory – Number of Edge nodes deployed per member state.
fields.definition:
This data is based on surveys completed in two data collection phases by 300 respondents from 10 member states  with 30 respondents from each member states in firms of more than 50 employees. In September 2023, the Edge observatory data collection 1  included respondents from  France, Germany, Italy, Netherlands, Spain, Sweden, Czech Republic, Poland, Romania and Estonia. The second Edge observatory data collection took place in June 2024 and included respondents from Austria, Belgium, Denmark, Finland, Greece, Ireland, Portugal, Hungary, Latvia, and Lithuania.
Dedicated reports and country fiches per Member state are available at the Edge observatory website.

Methodology: Data for the other member states has been estimated as presented in the Edge Observatory methodology.

Edge Deployment Data Reports:

  • 1st Edge Deployment Data Report (September 2023) Row data:  IDC Survey Data,  Targets 2030 calculations)
  • 2nd Edge Deployment Data Report (March 2024) [International comparison]
  • 3rd Edge Deployment Data Report (June 2024) Row data:  IDC Survey Data, Targets 2030 calculations)
  • 4th Edge Deployment Data Report (August 2024) [International comparison]

fields.label: Broadband coverage in Europe studies for the European Commission by Omdia and Point Topic
fields.definition:
All data by the Broadband Coverage in Europe study is available at this link and also at Eurostat, table ISOC_CBT: Broadband internet coverage by technology

Data collected by Omdia and Point Topic and verified by the national regulatory authorities (by data experts appointed by the members of the Communications Committee in every Member State).

Methodology: 

Broadband Coverage in Europe 2024 (reference year of the data)

Broadband Coverage in Europe 2023 (reference year of the data)

Broadband Coverage in Europe 2022 (reference year of the data)

Broadband Coverage in Europe 2021 (reference year of the data)

Broadband Coverage in Europe 2020 (reference year of the data)
